title: Astrology and Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Astrology & Evangelion?!             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jul 05, 2006 9:54 am I was reading through the basics of astrology today and came across something neat. I haven't thought about this long enough to extract any real significance from it (hence why I'm posting this in the chat sub-forum), but it's a pretty neat connection; in astrology the black moon (the lunar apogee) is associated with Lilith! Lilith and the Moon's nodes are exceptions: these points are based on astronomical data and consequently find more acceptance in astrological circles. The moonâ€™s nodes, which are always exactly opposed, are those points where the paths of the moon and the earth cross. Most astrologers see these points as being of karmic significance: The ascending or northern node is said to represent the spiritual development called for in this life, while the descending or southern node represents experience gathered during previous lives. Another point of view regards the northern node as a point of meeting, signifying important personal ties or connections, while the south node indicates separation. The well-known German astrologer Thomas Ring taught that the northern node shows where subconscious contents can become accessible, whereas the southern node shows where conscious awareness can sink back into the depths of the unconscious.

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Sun Jul 09, 2006 4:03 pm Brother, firstly, YAY, so cool to see you again. 2ndly, laimen terms please....

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Jul 09, 2006 4:16 pm O_O ZOMG! O_O I join Titus, though I have a little idea of what you are saying. Interesting, it is... *FauÃŸtin is trying to understand what he read a minute ago*

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Jul 09, 2006 4:18 pm It is intresting, but not wholly surprising-Anno drew from Astrology for inspiration. Now, I'm not calling Anno a master astrologist, but it's not like he can't find it online or in a book...

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Jul 09, 2006 5:39 pm The Dark Moon in this context is said to be representative of a voluntary inwash of the outside world. In the context of Third Impact this may apply to those confronted with a siren, but the connection still feels like one of those "in name only" things if on purpose at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Titus » Sun Jul 09, 2006 7:36 pm I forgot how hard it was to comprehend the level of intelligence on this site...... Too many big words.....must draw pictures.....

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Wed Jul 12, 2006 5:37 am Wayyyyyyyyyyy too much thinking. Even in that picture... Though I already knew that Garfield the Cat had a ton to do with Evangelion and third impact. Originally, he created the black moon, but became too obsessed with himself to actually control it, so he passed the responsibility on to Al Gore, who worked with Lilith to control the moon. Either way, ZZW (Zugzwang) will always freak me out and continue to give my quote some sort of truth behind it. Good show ZZW, good show.

Article: Astrology and Evangelion
[]: Astrology & Evangelion?!             Quote Post by Zuggy » Wed Jul 05, 2006 9:54 am I was reading through the basics of astrology today and came across something neat. I haven't thought about this long enough to extract any real significance from it (hence why I'm posting this in the chat sub-forum), but it's a pretty neat connection; in astrology the black moon (the lunar apogee) is associated with Lilith! Lilith and the Moon's nodes are exceptions: these points are based on astronomical data and consequently find more acceptance in astrological circles. The moonâ€™s nodes, which are always exactly opposed, are those points where the paths of the moon and the earth cross. Most astrologers see these points as being of karmic significance: The ascending or northern node is said to represent the spiritual development called for in this life, while the descending or southern node represents experience gathered during previous lives. Another point of view regards the northern node as a point of meeting, signifying important personal ties or connections, while the south node indicates separation. The well-known German astrologer Thomas Ring taught that the northern node shows where subconscious contents can become accessible, whereas the southern node shows where conscious awareness can sink back into the depths of the unconscious.
